isolation.environment: local Next.js + PartyKit stack with live browser task surface

steps.action: Measure the visible delivery-loop transition from inside the page clock while posting a daemon event.
steps.expected: The visible `CI Gate` update lands within 1 second of the event emission.
steps.observed: The page-clock probe stored `startMs=37445.70` immediately before the daemon-event fetch and `ciGateMs=38075.30` when a `MutationObserver` first saw `CI Gate`, for a `629.60ms` visible update latency.

steps.action: Drop the PartyKit server under a live task page and bring it back.
steps.expected: The client reconnects to the same user channel without a manual refresh.
steps.observed: The probe logged `[broadcast] disconnected from channel: user:67DFFNToBBmnlKm4XPaTQ8QuBe6bWh2k`, then reopened `ws://127.0.0.1:1999/parties/main/user:67DFFNToBBmnlKm4XPaTQ8QuBe6bWh2k?...` and logged `[broadcast] connected to channel: user:67DFFNToBBmnlKm4XPaTQ8QuBe6bWh2k` after restart.

steps.action: Send a new delivery-loop broadcast after reconnection.
steps.expected: The reconnected page resumes invalidation and UI updates normally.
steps.observed: A direct signed broadcast produced the `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` frame and an immediate task refetch, and the UI returned to `Awaiting PR` with no manual refresh.

assertions.title: UI Query Invalidation on Broadcast Receipt

steps.action: Measure the gap between a received delivery-loop websocket frame and the next task refetch after reconnect.
steps.expected: The invalidation path starts a new request within 100ms of receiving the broadcast.
steps.observed: On the reconnected channel, the task refetch was observed `0.197ms` after the `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` frame.

steps.action: Measure full daemon-event to visible UI transition on the real task page.
steps.expected: The entire round trip completes within 1 second.
steps.observed: The page-clock visual latency probe measured `629.60ms` from daemon-event emission to the visible `CI Gate` state. The transport-only probe also showed `170.46ms` from event start to websocket frame and `398.66ms` from event start to the follow-up task fetch.

assertions.title: Heartbeat Catches Missed Events After Reconnection

steps.action: Disconnect PartyKit, mutate delivery-loop state underneath the page, and wait for the heartbeat fallback.
steps.expected: The client recovers the missed status through its fallback fetch path instead of staying stale.
steps.observed: While the websocket was disconnected, the workflow state was staged to `CI Gate` in Postgres. The page fired a task refetch `93.76ms` later and the UI moved from `Awaiting PR` to `CI Gate` before the socket returned.

evidence.note: The delivery-loop surface recovers missed state by refetching its status query; it does not use the chat `thread-replay` path described in the older generic cross-surface contract text.

assertions.title: Reconnection After WebSocket Drop Recovers State

steps.action: Restart PartyKit beneath an active task page and deliver a new broadcast afterward.
steps.expected: The page reconnects and receives new delivery-loop broadcasts normally.
steps.observed: After restart, the page re-established the same user-channel websocket, received a new `refetch:["delivery-loop"]` broadcast `9.25ms` after POST start, and immediately refetched task state.

summary: The end-to-end runtime contracts are now covered by live browser evidence. Delivery-loop status visibly updates in `629.6ms`, reconnect resumes the same PartyKit user channel, broadcasts still trigger near-immediate invalidation on that recovered channel, and the heartbeat fallback catches missed state while the socket is down.
